rows.notes: Clear, large title at top of page. Legible Latin heading, no amounts present and well separated from numeral columns.
Date line under the title. Handwriting slightly compressed but readable as '2d de Novbr 1830'; no monetary columns present on this line.
Section heading at left of page introducing receipts. Word clearly separated from the numeric columns on the right.

Right-hand number group reads as '821 7 0' based on column alignment: pounds column contains '821', shillings column '7', pence '0'. Description is partially faded/abbreviated; transcribed as 'Arrears p[?]' because the hand on the label is unclear.
Clear alignment in pounds/shillings/pence columns: '195' in pounds column, '10' in shillings, '8' pence. Description reads as Latin 'Anni praecedtes' (preceding year); script legible.
Pounds '4076' and shillings '10' are well aligned. The pence position contains only a halfpenny mark (loop like 'ob') with no whole pence digit — interpreted as 0 pence + 0.5 (halfpenny).

Underlined subtotal line (numerals only). Read as '5093' pounds, '16' shillings, '8' pence and a halfpenny sign. Column alignment for these digits is clear; minor uncertainty because the subtotal pence and fraction placement is slightly cramped.
Text 'Debita Anni praecedent.' is legible. Amount columns read '119' (pounds), '7' (shillings), '6' (pence) with clear column separation.

Another totals line with only numerals visible. Read as '5243' pounds and '4' shillings with a halfpenny mark in the pence column (no whole pence digit) → interpreted as 0 pence + 0.5. Slight uncertainty because the fraction sign is small but consistent with other halfpenny marks on the page.
Section heading introducing payments/expenditures for rations/scholars. Handwriting slightly flourishy but readable as Latin phrase; no numeric entries on the heading line.

Pounds and shillings align to '520' and '16' respectively. Pence area shows '2' with a following loop-like symbol interpreted as '3q' (three farthings) → 0.75. The fraction mark is somewhat ambiguous (could be 1/2), but the loop plus tail suggests three farthings; assigned moderate confidence.
Description 'Ordinaris' (ordinary expenses) is legible. Amount reads cleanly across the three columns as '619' / '3' / '7'.
Pounds '3804' and shillings '0' are clear. Pence column shows '9' with a halfpenny sign (loop) after it → interpreted as 9 pence + 0.5. Alignment with the pounds/shillings lines is consistent.

Subtotal line for this expenditure block appears as numerals only: '4944' pounds, '0' shillings, '7' pence and a small 'q' like symbol indicating a farthing (0.25). The farthing sign is faint but positioned exactly in the pence fraction location.
Line labeled 'Ex Receptis' matches earlier total. Numeric group read as '5243' / '4' / (halfpenny). The halfpenny sign is consistent with earlier halfpenny markings on receipts side.

Text appears to indicate amounts deducted; numeric columns mirror the '4944 0 7 1/4' subtotal above. Farthing (0.25) sign present and aligned in fraction location; the label is somewhat abbreviated in the manuscript.

Final balance line clearly written as 'Restet solvendum' with numerals '299' / '3' / '5' and a small farthing sign (0.25) after the pence digit. Column alignment is consistent; fraction sign is faint but distinct.
Section heading introducing the signatories. Handwritten phrase is somewhat flourishy but legible as a computation-certification line; no amounts present.
First signature/name line. Handwritten name is readable though surname abbreviation and title are slightly stylized; no numeric data on this line.
Second signature/attesting name. Hand is less clear; interpreted as 'J. Sewell' and an office/title following that is somewhat abbreviated. No amounts.
Name line fairly clear: 'J. Day Socius' (or similar). No money values present.
Longer signature with several flourishes; surname reading 'Wollocombe' and title approximated as 'Bursarium Seniorum'. Handwriting moderately ornate causing some uncertainty in exact spelling.
Handwritten name line; first name and surname are somewhat unclear (transcribed as 'Guiliman Andrew' based on letter shapes). No numeric content; moderate uncertainty due to script style.

Final attesting name line is heavily flourishy and compressed. I transcribe as best-fit 'Robertturn J. Sutton, Bursarium Seniorum' — significant uncertainty in exact spelling of the first element; no amounts present.
